On February 8, 2022, Peloton Interactive, Inc. issued a press release announcing a restructuring plan.
Peloton Interactive, Inc. filed a Form 8-K on February 8, 2022, regarding its financial results for the quarter ended December 31, 2021, and the announcement of a restructuring plan.
John Foley, the Company's Co-Founder and former CEO, was appointed Executive Chair of the Board on February 7, 2022.
On February 7, 2022, the Board increased its size from seven to nine directors and appointed Angel L. Mendez and Jonathan Mildenhall to the Board.

Erik Blachford notified the Board of his resignation as a Class I director and from the Compensation and NCGR Committees, effective February 8, 2022. His resignation was not the result of any disagreement with the Company.
Barry McCarthy previously served on the boards of Chegg, Eventbrite, MSD Acquisition Corp, Pandora, and Rent the Runway.
On February 7, 2022, the Board appointed Barry McCarthy as Chief Executive Officer and President of Peloton Interactive, Inc., effective February 9, 2022, succeeding John Foley.
On February 7, 2022, the Board increased its size from seven to nine directors and appointed Angel L. Mendez and Jonathan Mildenhall to the Board.
Barry McCarthy has served on the boards of Spotify and Instacart.

Under his Offer Letter dated February 7, 2022, Barry McCarthy is eligible for an annual base salary of $1,000,000, a stock option award to purchase 8,000,000 shares of Class A Common Stock, up to $150,000 relocation reimbursement, and up to $30,000 legal cost reimbursement.
Barry McCarthy has served on the boards of Spotify and Instacart.
Barry McCarthy served as Spotify's Chief Financial Officer from 2015 to January 2020.
Barry McCarthy previously served on the boards of Chegg, Eventbrite, MSD Acquisition Corp, Pandora, and Rent the Runway.

In connection with Barry McCarthy's appointment as President, William Lynch will transition from the position of President to a non-executive director on the Board.
From 1999 to 2010, Barry McCarthy served as CFO and Principal Accounting Officer of Netflix, Inc.
Barry McCarthy previously served on the boards of Chegg, Eventbrite, MSD Acquisition Corp, Pandora, and Rent the Runway.
Hisao Kushi serves as the Chief Legal and Culture Officer for Peloton Interactive, Inc.
Peloton utilizes Tonic as a manufacturing partner to support its growth.
John Foley transitioned from his role as CEO of Peloton to assume the role of Executive Chair of the Board of Directors.

Peloton provided Q3 FY2022 guidance expecting approximately 2.93 million ending Connected Fitness Subscriptions, total revenue between $950 million and $1 billion, a gross margin of approximately 23%, and Adjusted EBITDA between negative $140 million and negative $125 million.
Peloton decided to wind down the development of Peloton Output Park, believing that Tonic and third-party manufacturing partners can support growth for the next few years.
William Lynch transitioned from his role as President of Peloton to become a non-executive director on the Board.
John Foley assumed the role of Executive Chair of the Board of Directors at Peloton.
Barry McCarthy was appointed as the CEO of Peloton to lead the company's next stage of growth.

For the second quarter of fiscal year 2022, ending February 8, 2022, Peloton reported that Connected Fitness Subscriptions grew 66% to 2.77 million, paid Digital Subscriptions grew 38% to 862 thousand, and total Members grew to over 6.6 million. Total revenue grew 6% to $1,133.9 million, while the company saw a Net Loss of $439.4 million ($1.39 per diluted share) and Adjusted EBITDA of negative $266.5 million with a margin of negative 23.5%.

For the three months ended December 31, 2021, Peloton reported total revenue of $1,133.9 million, Connected Fitness segment revenue of $796.4 million, Subscription revenue of $337.5 million, and gross profit of $280.2 million (24.7% of revenue). The company had a net loss of $439.4 million and cash and cash equivalents of $1.6 billion.

Peloton announced a meaningful reduction of its workforce impacting nearly all operations and almost all levels as part of a comprehensive restructuring program to align with the post-COVID demand landscape.

Peloton's total revenue growth in Q2 2022 was driven by higher Subscription revenue and the contribution from Precor, though offset by a decline in Connected Fitness Product sales.
Peloton announced the appointment of Jonathan Mildenhall as a new Board member.
Peloton officially announced the launch of Peloton Guide, its first connected fitness strength product, on April 5, priced at $495.
William Lynch transitioned to the role of non-executive director on the Board of Peloton.
Peloton announced the appointment of Angel Mendez and Jonathan Mildenhall as new Board members.
John Foley co-founded Peloton, starting as a five-person fitness group aiming to bring boutique fitness into the home.

Peloton updated its full FY2022 guidance to expect approximately 3.0 million ending Connected Fitness Subscriptions, total revenue between $3.7 billion and $3.8 billion (down from the prior $5.4 billion guidance given in August 2021), a gross margin of approximately 28%, and Adjusted EBITDA between negative $675 million and negative $625 million.
Erik Blachford stepped down from his role on the Board of Peloton.

relations.context: Barry McCarthy previously served in senior leadership roles at Spotify and Netflix.
Peloton expects its restructuring initiatives to yield at least $800 million in annual run-rate cost savings through operating expense efficiencies and improvements in Connected Fitness gross margin.
Peloton Interactive, Inc. will optimize its logistics network by reducing its owned and operated warehousing and delivery footprint.

Peloton Interactive, Inc. expects to achieve at least $800 million of annual run-rate cost savings through operating expense efficiencies and significant margin improvement in its Connected Fitness category.
Peloton Interactive, Inc. is winding down the development of its Peloton Output Park (POP) manufacturing plan, resulting in $60 million in restructuring capital expenditures.
On February 8, 2022, Peloton Interactive, Inc. announced a comprehensive program to reduce costs and drive growth, profitability, and free cash flow.
Peloton Interactive, Inc. rescheduled its second quarter 2022 earnings conference call from 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time to 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time on the day of the announcement.
Peloton Interactive, Inc. will reduce its planned capital expenditures in 2022 by approximately $150 million.
The workforce reduction will occur across nearly all business operations, resulting in the reduction of approximately 2,800 global positions, with corporate positions reduced by approximately 20%.
Peloton Interactive, Inc. is right-sizing the organization by enacting a workforce reduction across nearly all business operations.
The restructuring program is expected to result in approximately $130 million in cash charges related to severance and other exit/restructuring activities, and $80 million in non-cash charges.
